title: Green Card News — Immigration News & Alerts — ELLIS PORTER
content:
The Department of Labor (DOL) has published a new regulation that substantially increases the prevailing wage levels used when filing H-1B and E-3 work visa applications and PERM labor certification applications. This new DOL rule is effective immediately.
However, since DOL circumvented the normal rulemaking process to roll out this new rule with very weak legal justifications for doing so, we expect this new rule will be challenged in court.w rule with very weak legal justifications for doing so, we expect this new rule will be challenged in court.
Green cards containing date errors are being recalled for some spouses of US citizens.
Date movement is slowing or has stopped in all categories.  Learn more in our analysis of the June 2018 visa bulletin.
Who are the advancers for May?  See our analysis on what lies ahead for priority dates.
EB-1 adjustment applicants need to plan for a potentially lengthy retrogression. Get our analysis of the latest visa bulletin.
The April visa bulletin brings important news for the EB-1 preference category.
March priority dates, you could say, continue to steadily march forward.  Get our analysis of the latest visa bulletin.
2018 continues with forward movement.  Downgrading to EB-3 China could be advantageous for some.
What made 2017 a Year to Remember in Immigration Law.
2018 starts off with no retrogression in priority dates.
Reports indicate a growing trend of delays for I-765 and I-131 applications.
Beneficiaries of employment-based green cards will now be kept better informed.
The calendar year ends with forward movement in many categories.
Dates march forward at a slower pace than last month.
Interviews for employment-based green cards are rolling out.  Here's how to prepare.
Delays and interviews ahead for adjustment of status applicants.
The final Visa Bulletin for FY 2107 was just released and it contains a few surprises.
A confusing exchange while applying for a driver's license may result in deportation for an Illinois woman.
Newly designed Green Cards and EAD's go into production today and will include enhanced security features.  Employers should expect to see the updated design during the Form I-9 completion process.
